protocol_definition.termination: Stop after adaptation and Writer output or when the interaction budget is reached.

1. Verify current Zoom/Google Workspace subscription tiers to determine if AI features are already included.
2. Request trial access to Otter.ai, Fireflies.ai, and Tactiq to test speaker identification accuracy and action-item extraction with real lab meetings.

The AI meeting-assistant market has matured rapidly through 2025–2026, with tools now offering real-time transcription, speaker identification, action-item extraction, and integration with major video platforms. For a small university research lab (12 members, budget-sensitive, using Zoom and Google Meet), the key differentiators are: (a) whether the tool is platform-integrated (bundled with Zoom or Google Workspace) or standalone (third-party), (b) pricing model (per-seat vs. included), (c) data retention and privacy controls, and (d) export capabilities. Below is a comparison of four current options.

| **Otter.ai** | Standalone | Zoom, Google Meet, Microsoft Teams, Webex | Real-time transcription, speaker identification, automated summaries, action items, searchable transcripts, export (PDF, TXT, SRT, CSV) | Free tier (limited 300 min/month); Pro ~$16.99/user/month (billed annually); Business ~$30/user/month. Source: otter.ai official site (retrieved 2026-07-21) | Otter stores transcripts on its servers; data retention period not publicly clear from search results. Source: zoom.us pricing pages (retrieved 2026-07-21) | Zoom's AI Companion processes meeting data; Zoom states it does not use customer audio/video for training without consent. | **Fireflies.ai** | Standalone | Zoom, Google Meet, Teams, Webex, RingCentral | Automatic transcription, speaker identification, action items, search, integrations with CRM/project tools | Free tier (limited); Pro ~$10/user/month (billed annually); Business ~$19/user/month; Enterprise custom. 3. Market Trends and Differentiators

- **Platform-integrated tools (Zoom AI Companion)** are gaining traction as free add-ons to existing subscriptions, eliminating per-seat costs but locking users into a single platform. For labs already on paid Zoom plans, this is the most cost-effective option.
- **Standalone tools (Otter, Fireflies, Tactiq, Fathom)** offer cross-platform flexibility and richer export/action-item features but add per-user subscription costs. Fathom's free unlimited-transcript tier is a notable differentiator for budget-sensitive teams.
- **Privacy and consent** are critical differentiators for academic settings. Tools differ in whether they require a bot to join meetings (Fireflies, Fathom) vs. running as a browser extension without a bot (Tactiq) vs. native integration (Zoom AI Companion). Tactiq's local-storage option and lack of a bot may be preferable for privacy-conscious labs.
- **Action-item extraction** is now a standard feature across all tools, but accuracy varies and is not independently benchmarked. Labs should trial tools with real meeting recordings.
- **Export capabilities** vary: Otter offers the widest range of export formats (PDF, TXT, SRT, CSV); Tactiq integrates directly with Google Docs and Notion; Fathom offers highlight reels and CRM integrations.
